claim_boundary: Tier 7.7c is a contract/pre-registration gate only. It performs no long-run scoring, freezes no baseline, claims no public usefulness, and authorizes no hardware/native transfer.

classification.next_gate: Tier 7.7d - Standardized Long-Run / Failure-Localization Scoring Gate

diagnostic_questions.failure_interpretation: short-run delayed scalar recurrence benefit only
diagnostic_questions.primary_metrics: per-seed test_mse, tail_mse, v2.3/v2.5 ratio by length
diagnostic_questions.question: Does the Mackey-Glass improvement persist or grow as the stream length increases?

diagnostic_questions.failure_interpretation: requires richer state reconstruction or multichannel/state-space interface before mechanism layering
diagnostic_questions.primary_metrics: Lorenz v2.5-vs-v2.3 delta, ESN gap, state-disabled and prediction-disabled controls
diagnostic_questions.question: Is Lorenz flat because the current one-dimensional causal interface lacks enough state reconstruction?

diagnostic_questions.failure_interpretation: requires targeted memory-depth/readout repair rather than more high-level planning mechanics
diagnostic_questions.primary_metrics: NARMA10 v2.5-vs-v2.3 delta, lag-depth diagnostics, memory/prediction-disabled controls
diagnostic_questions.question: Is NARMA10 flat because the current memory/readout path lacks sufficient nonlinear memory depth?

diagnostic_questions.failure_interpretation: CRA remains below standard sequence baselines on this suite; broad usefulness claim stays blocked
diagnostic_questions.primary_metrics: rank, geomean_mse, train_mse/test_mse gap, tail_mse, parameter/disclosed feature budget
diagnostic_questions.question: Do ESN/online-linear/ridge baselines win because of richer state, stronger readout, or simple linear fit advantages?

diagnostic_questions.failure_interpretation: apparent long-run improvement is not causally specific enough for promotion
diagnostic_questions.primary_metrics: target-shuffle margin, time-shuffle margin, planning-disabled margin, state-disabled margin
diagnostic_questions.question: Do target/time/state/planning shams stay separated at longer lengths?

contract.hypothesis: If v2.5 captures useful general temporal structure, its aggregate advantage versus v2.3 should persist or grow at 16000 and 32000 steps without sham matching, and failure decomposition should show which mechanisms help which task family.
leakage_rules: same causal observed stream only; no future target or test-label access
normalization fit on train prefix only
same seeds/tasks/splits for candidate and baselines
optional 50000-step run cannot replace required 8000/16000/32000 lengths
do not add or tune mechanisms inside the scoring gate

length_matrix.claim_use: reproduce Tier 7.7b locked score inside the long-run scoring output
test whether v2.5 signal persists after twice the locked 7.7b exposure
test whether v2.5 signal grows, plateaus, or collapses over longer exposure
optional stress only if finite/runtime budget permits; cannot replace required lengths

models.role: optional reviewer-defense baseline if dependency/runtime budget is locked

contract.next_gate: Tier 7.7d - Standardized Long-Run / Failure-Localization Scoring Gate
nonclaims: not a new score by itself
not external-baseline superiority unless 7.7d shows it
not language, broad reasoning, AGI, or ASI evidence
contract.null_hypothesis: The 7.7b gain is a Mackey-Glass-local short-run effect; Lorenz/NARMA10 and strong standard baselines remain better, or shams explain the apparent gain.

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: long-run standardized progress versus v2.3; still not external-baseline superiority unless baselines are beaten
pass_fail_criteria.rule: v2.5 improves v2.3 aggregate by >=10% at both 16000 and 32000 with paired support, while at least 2/3 tasks or tail metrics improve and shams stay separated

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: localized delayed scalar recurrence signal only
pass_fail_criteria.rule: Mackey-Glass remains improved but Lorenz/NARMA10 stay flat or worse

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: progress versus older CRA only; no broad usefulness or baseline-competitive claim
pass_fail_criteria.rule: v2.5 improves v2.3 but ESN/online-linear/ridge remain materially better on aggregate

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: no long-run usefulness upgrade; route to measured failure repair
pass_fail_criteria.rule: 7.7b aggregate improvement disappears at 16000 or 32000, or shams match the candidate

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: narrow paper to architecture/evidence/hardware substrate unless a future predeclared mechanism changes the scoreboard
pass_fail_criteria.rule: v2.5 fails to improve v2.3 at longer lengths and standard baselines dominate all required lengths

contract.question: Does the Tier 7.7b v2.5 standardized progress signal persist under longer streams, and what explains the Lorenz/NARMA10 plus external-baseline gap?

shams.purpose: audit causal ordering and future-label absence

contract.split: chronological train 65%, test 35%, tail = final quartile of test suffix
